2026-04-21T05:38:36.0639138Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T05:38:36.0639434Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. This push introduced the package files for the first time into the repository, including `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, generated sources, and tests.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **\"Key concepts\" section is a stub** — it only links to the generic Azure SDK intro page; it should describe Blueprint-specific concepts such as blueprints, artifacts (policy/role-assignment/template), published blueprints, and assignments\n- **\"Examples\" section contains no code** — it only links to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL; it should include at least one concrete code snippet showing how to create/list/delete a blueprint or assignment\n- **No `Samples/` test file with `#region Snippet:` annotations** — the tests directory has `TestCase/` files but no `Samples/Sample*.cs` file; snippet-backed examples cannot be generated without this\n- **\"Authenticate the Client\" section lacks example code** — it defers entirely to a quickstart link; a short inline snippet showing `ArmClient` initialization would improve discoverability\n- **\"Troubleshooting\" section is generic** — does not mention Blueprint-specific error scenarios (e.g., assignment conflicts, locked resources)\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/README.md` — edit\n\nReplace the hollow `## Key concepts` section with Blueprint-specific content:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nAzure Blueprints consists of:\n\n- **Blueprint** (`BlueprintResource`) — a reusable definition of Azure resources, policies, and role assignments that can be applied to a subscription or management group.\n- **Artifact** (`BlueprintArtifactResource`) — a component of a blueprint; can be a policy assignment, role assignment, or ARM template.\n- **Published Blueprint** (`PublishedBlueprintResource`) — an immutable snapshot of a blueprint at a specific version.\n- **Assignment** (`AssignmentResource`) — the application of a published blueprint to a scope (subscription or management group).\n- **Assignment Operation** (`AssignmentOperationResource`) — tracks the status of an in-progress or completed assignment deployment.\n```\n\nReplace the hollow `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create a blueprint\n\n```C# Snippet:Blueprint_CreateBlueprint\n```\n\n### Publish a blueprint version\n\n```C# Snippet:Blueprint_PublishBlueprint\n```\n\n### Create a blueprint assignment\n\n```C# Snippet:Blueprint_CreateAssignment\n```\n```\n\n**File:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/README.md` — edit the \"Authenticate the Client\" section\n\n```markdown\n### Authenticate the client\n\n```C# Snippet:Blueprint_AuthenticateClient\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nCreate the file `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/tests/Samples/Sample1_Blueprint.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint.Models;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_Blueprint\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task AuthenticateClient()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_AuthenticateClient\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateBlueprint()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_CreateBlueprint\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\";\n            string blueprintName = \"myBlueprint\";\n\n            // Blueprints can be scoped to a subscription or management group.\n            // Here we use a subscription scope.\n            var scope = $\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\";\n            var blueprintCollection = client.GetBlueprints(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\n\n            var blueprintData = new BlueprintData\n            {\n                Description = \"My sample blueprint\",\n                TargetScope = BlueprintTargetScope.Subscription,\n            };\n\n            var operation = await blueprintCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\n                blueprintName,\n                blueprintData);\n\n            BlueprintResource blueprint = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created blueprint: {blueprint.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task PublishBlueprint()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_PublishBlueprint\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\";\n            string blueprintName = \"myBlueprint\";\n            string version = \"v1\";\n\n            var scope = $\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\";\n            var blueprintCollection = client.GetBlueprints(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\n            BlueprintResource blueprint = await blueprintCollection.GetAsync(blueprintName);\n\n            var publishedCollection = blueprint.GetPublishedBlueprints();\n            var publishedData = new PublishedBlueprintData();\n\n            var operation = await publishedCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\n                version,\n                publishedData);\n\n            PublishedBlueprintResource published = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Published blueprint version: {published.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateAssignment()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_CreateAssignment\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\";\n            string assignmentName = \"myAssignment\";\n            string blueprintId =\n                $\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/myBlueprint/versions/v1\";\n\n            var scope = $\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\";\n            var assignmentCollection = client.GetAssignments(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\n\n            var assignmentData = new AssignmentData(\n                new AssignmentIdentity(AssignmentIdentityType.SystemAssigned),\n                Azure.Core.AzureLocation.EastUS,\n                blueprintId,\n                new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary\u003cstring, AssignmentParameterValue\u003e(),\n                new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary\u003cstring, AssignmentResourceGroup\u003e());\n\n            var operation = await assignmentCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\n                assignmentName,\n                assignmentData);\n\n            AssignmentResource assignment = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created assignment: {assignment.Data.Name}, Status: {assignment.Data.ProvisioningState}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nFor each snippet:\n- `Blueprint_AuthenticateClient` → README section \"Authenticate the client\"\n- `Blueprint_CreateBlueprint` → README section \"Examples \u003e Create a blueprint\"\n- `Blueprint_PublishBlueprint` → README section \"Examples \u003e Publish a blueprint version\"\n- `Blueprint_CreateAssignment` → README section \"Examples \u003e Create a blueprint assignment\"\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nCurrent README sections vs required:\n\n| Required Section | Present? | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Getting started \u003e Install the package | ✅ 2026-04-21T05:41:39.5634619Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5634728Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint`
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5634873Z **Service directory:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/`
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5634975Z **Triggered by:** c946b7a4c (#58424) by @azure-sdk
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5634978Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635042Z ## What Changed
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635045Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635742Z The `Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint` package was released as `1.0.0-beta.6` and the version was subsequently bumped to `1.0.0-beta.7 (Unreleased)`. This push introduced the package files for the first time into the repository, including `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, generated sources, and tests.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635746Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635806Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5635810Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5636514Z - **"Key concepts" section is a stub** — it only links to the generic Azure SDK intro page; it should describe Blueprint-specific concepts such as blueprints, artifacts (policy/role-assignment/template), published blueprints, and assignments
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5637133Z - **"Examples" section contains no code** — it only links to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL; it should include at least one concrete code snippet showing how to create/list/delete a blueprint or assignment
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5637781Z - **No `Samples/` test file with `#region Snippet:` annotations** — the tests directory has `TestCase/` files but no `Samples/Sample*.cs` file; snippet-backed examples cannot be generated without this
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638308Z - **"Authenticate the Client" section lacks example code** — it defers entirely to a quickstart link; a short inline snippet showing `ArmClient` initialization would improve discoverability
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638709Z - **"Troubleshooting" section is generic** — does not mention Blueprint-specific error scenarios (e.g., assignment conflicts, locked resources)
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638714Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638777Z <details>
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638956Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5638960Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639203Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639312Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639383Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639387Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639588Z **File:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/README.md` — edit
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639592Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639743Z Replace the hollow `## Key concepts` section with Blueprint-specific content:
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639746Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639805Z ```markdown
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639868Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639871Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639944Z Azure Blueprints consists of:
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5639953Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5640434Z - **Blueprint** (`BlueprintResource`) — a reusable definition of Azure resources, policies, and role assignments that can be applied to a subscription or management group.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5640803Z - **Artifact** (`BlueprintArtifactResource`) — a component of a blueprint; can be a policy assignment, role assignment, or ARM template.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5641127Z - **Published Blueprint** (`PublishedBlueprintResource`) — an immutable snapshot of a blueprint at a specific version.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5641483Z - **Assignment** (`AssignmentResource`) — the application of a published blueprint to a scope (subscription or management group).
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5641841Z - **Assignment Operation** (`AssignmentOperationResource`) — tracks the status of an in-progress or completed assignment deployment.
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5641898Z ```
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5641906Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5642046Z Replace the hollow `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder:
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5642049Z 
2026-04-21T05:41:39.5642107Z ```markdown

2026-04-21T05:41:39.5642724Z
